articles.title: HOW PINTEREST OPTIMIZED VIDEO PLAYBACK
articles.summary: Pinterest optimized video playback by embedding video manifest files directly into their API responses, removing the extra network requests needed to initiate playback. This reduced startup latency, as the video player could immediately access the necessary metadata. To fix the increased API response time caused by this change, Pinterest implemented a MemCache layer to cache frequently accessed manifest files, which led to a 36.7% reduction in startup latency on iOS.

articles.title: PARSING JSON IN 500 LINES OF RUST
articles.summary: A JSON parser built in 500 lines of Rust is able to convert JSON text into a Rust enum representing the JSON structure and handle various data types and errors. Performance testing revealed speeds comparable to other parsers. Surprisingly, the parser has a performance boost when run with sudo.

articles.title: MY LLM CODEGEN WORKFLOW ATM
articles.summary: This developer uses LLMs in three steps: brainstorming a detailed specification, creating a plan broken down into small, testable steps, and executing the plan using an LLM code generation tool like Claude or Aider. For existing codebases, he uses tools like Repomix to provide context to the LLM for tasks such as code review and test generation.

articles.title: WE SWITCHED FROM JAVA TO GO AND DON'T REGRET IT
articles.summary: Glasskube, creators of a Kubernetes package manager, announced the release of their open-source software distribution platform, Distr, on GitHub. The company switched from Java to Go for Distr, because it has much faster startup times (milliseconds vs. seconds) and lighter resource consumption.

articles.title: THOUGHTS ON DAYLIGHT COMPUTER
articles.summary: The Daylight Computer (DC-1) is a grayscale tablet designed for outdoor use, with sunlight readability and nighttime amber backlight. The main pro of the DC-1's design is that it leads to a better relationship with technology and the outdoors.

articles.title: STATE OF JAVA 2025
articles.summary: In this webinar, Java experts from Azul, Expedia, and evolutionnext discussed the latest Java ecosystem trends, as reflected in the State of Java Survey [9] conducted by Azul. Watch now to learn about the impact of AI, the imminent arrival of the next Java LTS release, and

articles.title: SCRIPTON
articles.summary: Scripton is a new Python IDE offering real-time data visualization directly within the coding environment. It uses plotting libraries like Plotly and Observable Plot for creating 2D graphics and interactive UI elements.

articles.title: THE TF2 SDK HAS ARRIVED!
articles.summary: The Team Fortress 2 (TF2) SDK has been released, granting modders access to the game's client and server code. This allows for the creation of entirely new games based on TF2's engine.

articles.title: VISPREX
articles.summary: Visprex is an open-source, in-browser data visualization tool for CSV files. It helps create histograms and scatterplots.

articles.title: A YEAR OF UV: PROS, CONS, AND SHOULD YOU MIGRATE
articles.summary: "uv” is a Python project management tool. uv's strengths are in its streamlined Python installation process across different operating systems, its dependency management, and its ease of use. After using it for a year, this developer believes that “uv” is the best tool to use for every Python project if possible.

articles.title: A NEW INTERPRETER IN PYTHON 3.14 DELIVERS A FREE SPEED BOOST
articles.summary: Python 3.14 will include a new interpreter offering up to a 30% performance increase for existing code without requiring any code changes, with a beta release expected in May 2025. This is going to happen by an optimization that the C compiler performs on CPython code, which speeds up the way the interpreter dispatches its bytecode instructions.

articles.title: KAFKA AT THE LOW END: HOW BAD CAN IT GET?
articles.summary: Kafka is a poor choice for low-volume job queues due to its potential for unfair job distribution among workers, leading to some workers being idle while others are overloaded.

articles.title: I'M GLAD AI DIDN'T EXIST WHEN I LEARNED TO CODE
articles.summary: A high school programmer is glad they learned to code without AI because, while it was frustrating at times, the process of debugging and problem-solving let them understand code much better overall.

articles.title: TESTING 10 JAVASCRIPT FRAMEWORKS ON THEIR HTML DEFAULTS
articles.summary: A web developer tested the default HTML output of ten popular JavaScript frameworks and found that many produced invalid or suboptimal HTML.

articles.title: STAFF PROJECTS: PT 3. BREAKING INTO STAFF ENGINEERING
articles.summary: Staff Engineers drive high-impact, multi-stakeholder projects that require strategic thinking, leadership, and execution at scale, while influencing the organization in a direction that delivers business value.
